snp5.2:1.1: With what is the world shrouded?
snp5.2:1.3: Because of what doesn’t it shine?
snp5.2:1.4:
With what is it smeared?
Tell me.
snp5.2:1.5:
What is its great danger & fear?
The Buddha:
snp5.2:2.1: With ignorance the world is shrouded.
snp5.2:2.3: Because of stinginess, heedlessness, it doesn’t shine.
snp5.2:2.4: With longing it’s smeared—I tell you.
snp5.2:2.5:
Suffering-stress:
its great danger & fear.
Ajita:
snp5.2:3.1: They flow every which way, the streams.
snp5.2:3.5:
with what are they finally stopped?
The Buddha:
snp5.2:4.1: Whatever streams there are in the world:
snp5.2:4.5:
with discernment they’re finally stopped.
Ajita:
snp5.2:5.5:
where are they brought to a halt?
The Buddha:
snp5.2:6.4: are brought to a halt without trace:
snp5.2:6.6:
they’re here brought to a halt.
Ajita:
snp5.2:7.1: Those here who have fathomed the Dhamma,
snp5.2:7.2: those who are learners, those who are run-of-the-mill:
snp5.2:7.3: When you, dear sir, astute, are asked this,
snp5.2:7.4:
tell me their manner of life.
The Buddha:
snp5.2:8.1: He should not hanker for sensual pleasures,
snp5.2:8.4: he, the monk, should wander mindfully.
